mechanism: Fiscal dominance captures the long end of the yield curve, making 30Y correlation with equities structurally positive (both driven by fiscal dynamics) while the front end remains monetary-policy-driven and negatively correlated. This produces the observed maturity-dependent bifurcation.
regime_dependence: Requires persistent deficits above ~4.5% GDP AND the central bank not absorbing net issuance. Currently both conditions are met. Would be disrupted by fiscal consolidation or renewed large-scale QE.
